diversification.title: Diversification
diversification.body: Don't put all your eggs in one basket. If you own 10 companies across different sectors and one goes bankrupt, you lose 10%. If you only own one, you lose 100%. Diversification doesn't eliminate risk — it spreads it out.
diversification.tip: Check your portfolio today: is more than 30% in a single sector?

dca.title: Dollar Cost Averaging
dca.body: Investing the same amount every month, regardless of whether the market goes up or down. When it drops, you buy more shares for the same money. When it rises, your shares are already worth more. Over time, your average price optimizes itself.
dca.tip: Set a fixed monthly amount and automate it. Discipline beats timing.

dividends.title: Dividends
dividends.body: Some companies pay you just for holding their stock. That payment is called a dividend. Mature companies (Coca-Cola, J&J, Apple) typically pay 1-4% a year. Automatically reinvesting dividends can double your return over 20 years.
dividends.tip: Look for companies with 10+ years of paying and raising dividends.

peRatio.title: P/E Ratio
peRatio.body: The Price/Earnings (P/E) ratio compares a stock's price to what the company earns. A P/E of 20 means you pay $20 for every $1 of profit. There's no universal 'good' number — always compare it against the sector and the company's own history.
peRatio.tip: A very high P/E doesn't mean expensive if the company is growing fast. Context beats the number.

moat.title: Competitive Advantage
moat.body: A 'moat' is what makes a company hard to copy. It can be a brand (Apple), a user network (Visa), low costs (Walmart), or patents (Pfizer). Companies with a strong moat protect their margins over time.
moat.tip: Ask yourself: what would stop a competitor with $1B from taking this market?

lossAversion.title: Loss Aversion
lossAversion.body: The human brain feels the pain of losing $100 twice as strongly as the pleasure of gaining $100. That's why we sell in panic during drops and buy in euphoria during rallies. Recognizing this bias is the first step to not acting on it.
lossAversion.tip: Before selling during a drop, ask yourself: did the business fundamentals actually change?

rebalancing.title: Rebalancing
rebalancing.body: If you started with 60% stocks / 40% bonds and stocks rallied hard, you might now be at 75/25 — more risk than you wanted. Rebalancing means selling what grew and buying what lagged to return to your target allocation.
rebalancing.tip: Rebalance 1-2 times a year, or whenever an asset drifts more than 5% from your target.

freeCashFlow.title: Free Cash Flow
freeCashFlow.body: Earnings can be manipulated through accounting. Free Cash Flow (FCF) doesn't lie — it's the real cash coming into the company after paying for operations and investments. Companies with growing FCF have real staying power.
freeCashFlow.tip: Look for companies whose FCF grows consistently faster than their revenue.

investmentHorizon.title: Investment Horizon
investmentHorizon.body: The market drops 10-20% on average once every 1-2 years. But over 10+ years, the S&P 500 has never lost money. Your investment horizon determines how much risk you can absorb. Over 20 years, drops are opportunities. Over 2 years, they're threats.
investmentHorizon.tip: Do you need this money in less than 3 years? It shouldn't be in stocks.

fomo.title: Financial FOMO
fomo.body: Fear Of Missing Out makes you buy at the top when everyone's excited. Bitcoin at $65K, hot stocks at their peak. FOMO is the most reliable sign that an asset is overvalued. The best time to get in is when nobody's talking about it.
fomo.tip: If you're seeing the asset in mainstream news and your family's WhatsApp group — be careful.

etfsVsStocks.title: ETFs vs Stocks
etfsVsStocks.body: An ETF (like VOO or SPY) tracks an entire index. With $1 you buy 500 companies. A single stock is a specific bet. Studies show 90% of active managers fail to beat the index over 15 years. ETFs are the smart starting point.
etfsVsStocks.tip: Start with broad ETFs. Add individual stocks only once you understand the business.

buyBusinesses.title: Buy Businesses, Not Tickers
buyBusinesses.body: Before buying a stock, ask yourself: do I understand how this company makes money? What makes it better than its competitors? Would I still want to own it if the market closed for 5 years? If you can't answer these questions, you probably shouldn't hold that position.
buyBusinesses.tip: Warren Buffett only invests in what he can understand in 5 minutes. Follow that rule.

dropsNormal.title: Drops Are Normal
dropsNormal.body: Since 1950, the S&P 500 has fallen more than 10% on average once a year. More than 20% every 3-5 years. Despite that, the index multiplied 200x over that period. Drops aren't the end of the market — they're the price of admission for long-term returns.
dropsNormal.tip: Keep a list of companies you'd want to buy when they drop. Drops are sales.

concentrationRisk.title: Concentration Risk
concentrationRisk.body: Having more than 20% in a single company or more than 40% in one sector is high concentration. If that company or sector collapses, so does your portfolio. The best portfolios hold positions of 3-10% each, spread across 5+ different sectors.
concentrationRisk.tip: Calculate your biggest position today. Is it more than 20% of your portfolio?

faq.q: How many messages can I send for free?
faq.a: The Free plan includes 15 messages every 24 hours. With Premium you get unlimited messages and access to all advanced features.

faq.q: How does the free 90-day Premium work?
faq.a: All new users automatically get 90 days of Premium for free, no credit card required. You can explore every feature without restrictions during that period.

faq.q: How do I import my portfolio?
faq.a: In the Portfolio section you can paste a screenshot from your broker (Ctrl+V / ⌘+V) or add positions manually. The AI reads the image and automatically extracts your positions, prices, and quantities.

faq.q: How do I see the full analysis of a stock?
faq.a: Tap or click any stock from Portfolio, Watchlist, or Chat to open its full profile. There you'll find the historical chart, income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow with real-time data.

faq.q: How does the earnings calendar work?
faq.a: In the Portfolio section you'll find a calendar showing the earnings report dates for your positions and Watchlist stocks. It helps you anticipate important price moves.

faq.q: Can I change the order of my stocks in the Watchlist?
faq.a: Yes. In the basic Watchlist view you can drag stock cards to reorder them however you like. The order saves automatically and persists across sessions.

It's a score (0-100) the AI calculates by analyzing your real behavior in the app: whether you panic during dips, whether you diversify well, whether you make long-term decisions. It evolves as you use the app and appears as a bar on your profile.

faq.q: Does the app sync between web and mobile?
faq.a: Yes. Your portfolio, watchlist, dark/light theme preference, and profile settings sync automatically between the web version and the mobile app in real time.

faq.q: How do I cancel my Premium subscription?
faq.a: Go to Profile → Subscription → Cancel. Premium access stays active until the end of the period you already paid for.

faq.q: How does the referral program work?
faq.a: Go to Profile → Referral program to get your unique link. For every friend who signs up and uses the app, you earn weeks or months of free Premium.

q1.question: Your portfolio drops 35% in 3 months due to a market crisis. What do you do?

options.B: Wait for it to recover, but don't buy more
options.C: Check if the fundamentals still hold up and stay invested
options.D: Take advantage to buy more at low prices

q2.question: What do you need this invested money for, and in how much time?

options.A: I might need it in less than 2 years
options.C: In 10+ years, for financial independence or retirement
options.D: I'm in no rush — it's to build long-term wealth

q3.question: Which of these concepts do you understand well enough to explain to someone else?

options.A: None confidently — I'm just starting out
options.D: Fundamental analysis, hedging with derivatives, market cycles

q4.question: You have $100,000 to invest. Which scenario do you prefer?

options.A: Earn a guaranteed $5K, with no chance of losing anything
options.B: Likely earn $15K, with a risk of losing $5K
options.C: Possibly earn $40K, with a risk of losing $20K
options.D: Possibly earn $120K, with a risk of losing it all

q5.question: How much time would you spend monitoring and managing your investments?

options.A: I'd rather have something automatic that needs no attention
options.B: A monthly or quarterly review seems enough to me
options.C: I like to check weekly and make adjustments when it's worth it
options.D: I'm willing to spend time on it daily — it's an active pursuit

moderate.desc: You seek a balance between growth and protection.

aggressive.label: Aggressive
aggressive.desc: You aim for maximum growth. You can tolerate high volatility.

1.title: Add your first position
1.desc: Look up a company you know — Apple, Tesla, Amazon — and add it to your simulated portfolio. No real money involved.

2.title: Here's what your portfolio looks like
2.desc: When you add a position, the app tracks its price in real time and shows you what your investment would be worth today. All simulated, no risk.

3.title: Ask the mentor your first question
3.desc: The AI mentor knows your profile. Ask something specific: Is now a good time to buy? How risky is this company?
3.cta: Go to chat with the mentor

1.title: Welcome to Nuvos AI
1.subtitle: Your AI investing mentor
1.desc: Nuvos AI teaches you to think like a professional investor. We don't tell you what to buy — we teach you to analyze, understand, and make decisions on your own. All data syncs in real time between the web and mobile app.
1.tip: 💡 Complete the initial profile so the AI adapts its answers to your level: Basic, Intermediate, or Advanced.

2.title: Your Dashboard
2.subtitle: The market's pulse, at a glance
2.desc: Home shows the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Jones indexes in real time with continuous scroll while the market is open (LIVE). Below you'll see your portfolio value with three key metrics: Today's performance, YTD (year to date), and Total.
2.tip: 💡 The "Trending up today" section automatically shows the 4 positions in your portfolio with the biggest gain of the day.

3.title: Chat with your AI Mentor
3.subtitle: Nuvos's main tool
3.desc: Ask about any company, ETF, concept, or strategy. The AI knows your risk profile, your real portfolio, and your investor level, and detects when your decisions contradict them. Every conversation updates your behavioral maturity bar.
3.tip: 💡 You can edit any message by clicking the pencil icon that appears next to the text.

4.title: Real-Time Portfolio
4.subtitle: Your investments with live data
4.desc: Add positions manually or by pasting a screenshot of your broker (Ctrl+V / ⌘+V) — the AI extracts the data automatically. Prices update every 30 seconds. Toggle between Basic and Advanced view to see Volume, Market Cap, P/E, Ex-Dividend, and 52-week range.
4.tip: 💡 The badge next to "My Portfolio" (USD, MXN, EUR…) shows your portfolio's currency. Click any position to see its full analysis.

5.title: Events Calendar
5.subtitle: Earnings, dividends, and ex-dividends
5.desc: The calendar in Watchlist automatically shows three types of events for all your positions and watchlist: Earnings (quarterly results), Ex-Dividend (deadline to receive the dividend), and Dividend payment. Tap any day to see the detail.
5.tip: 💡 Positions from your portfolio appear in bold green; watchlist ones in blue — so you can spot at a glance which ones affect you directly.

6.title: Watchlist
6.subtitle: Follow stocks without buying them
6.desc: Add any stock to your Watchlist to follow its price, news, and events in real time. In Advanced mode you get a full table with an after-hours column, market cap, P/E ratio, earnings date, and 52-week range — sortable by any column.
6.tip: 💡 Drag cards to reorder your watchlist. The order syncs automatically between web and mobile.

7.title: Learning & Tools
7.subtitle: Everything you need to invest better
7.desc: A library of financial concepts explained by AI, adapted to your level: ETFs, fundamental analysis, P/E ratio, DCA, Value Investing, and investor psychology. The weekly Screener (Intermediate+) analyzes the market and selects personalized opportunities every Monday.
7.tip: 💡 The Investors section shows you how the big funds invest — useful for spotting trends and validating your ideas.

8.title: Your Profile & Investor Maturity
8.subtitle: The AI that knows you as an investor
8.desc: The AI analyzes your real behavior — whether you panic, whether you diversify well, whether you think long-term — and assigns you an Investor Maturity score (0-100) that evolves over time. Your level (Basic, Intermediate, or Advanced) adapts the whole experience: analysis, chat answers, and visible columns.
8.tip: 💡 The behavioral risk bar in the side menu updates automatically with every conversation. You can change your level in Profile anytime.

freeFeatures: Up to 15 messages/day with the AI mentor
General notifications for your portfolio and watchlist
premiumFeatures: Chat without limits with your AI mentor, any time
Add as many stocks as you want, no limit
Upload a photo or PDF of your account and the AI builds your portfolio
We notify you before your companies report earnings
See how your money would have done in past crises (2008, COVID...)
The AI reviews your portfolio and tells you what to improve
Every Monday, 5 investment ideas picked for you
News about your stocks, summarized by AI in seconds
Every month we tell you whether you beat the market or not
Lessons tailored to the stocks you already hold
We notify you when something important happens with your money
Discover your investor style and how to improve it
duoFeatures: Everything in Premium, for both of you
Independent profile and portfolio for each person
Share with a family member or partner
Ideal for learning to invest together
pricingModal.title: Try Premium free for 1 month
pricingModal.footerNote: Free 30-day trial. Cancel anytime before it ends and you won't be charged. After the first month: {{price}}/month{{billing}}.

guidedSteps.allDoneTitle: 🎉 You know the whole app now!
guidedSteps.allDoneBody: You can explore on your own now. The guide will stay here if you need it.

chat.title: Your investing mentor
chat.what: This AI chat explains any financial concept in plain language at your level. You can ask about companies, how to get started, or what to do with your money.
chat.todo: Write your first question. Suggestion: "Where do I start investing with $500?"
chat.tip: There are no dumb questions here. The more you tell it about your goals, the better it can guide you.

learn.title: Your financial library
learn.what: Here you learn the key investing concepts in 30 seconds each. The AI explains them with real examples and no jargon.
learn.todo: Tap any topic tagged "For you". Recommended: Market Cap, Treasury Bills, or Diversification.
learn.tip: You don't need to learn it all today. With 2-3 topics a day, in a month you'll know more than most people.

portfolio.title: Your investment command center
portfolio.what: Here you log your stocks and the app calculates in real time how much you've gained or lost, and how you're doing against the market (S&P 500).
portfolio.todo: Tap the "+" button to add a position, or import a screenshot from your broker — the AI detects everything automatically.
portfolio.tip: If you don't have any stocks yet, add Apple (AAPL) with a $1 purchase price to see how the screen works.

watchlist.title: Your watchlist
watchlist.what: Here you save companies you're interested in but haven't bought yet. The app alerts you when they rise, fall, or have important news.
watchlist.todo: Search for 2-3 companies you know (Apple, Tesla, FEMSA) and add them with the "+" button. Then watch how they move.
watchlist.tip: Following companies without real money for 3-6 months is the best way to build your investing judgment.

notifications.title: Your personalized alerts
notifications.what: The app notifies you when your positions move significantly, there's relevant news for your stocks, or the market does something important.
notifications.todo: Check the alerts you already have. If you have positions in your portfolio, you should see movements here. Turn on push notifications if you haven't yet.
notifications.tip: It's not generic noise — every alert is filtered to what you actually hold in your portfolio and watchlist.

profile.title: Your investor profile
profile.what: Here the app learns about you: how much risk you tolerate, your time horizon, and how active you want to be. That personalizes everything else.
profile.todo: Check your knowledge level below and adjust it. As you learn more, update it so the app adapts with you.
profile.tip: Your profile isn't permanent. It evolves as you evolve as an investor.

paywallModal.heroFeature1: Know what to do with your money before the market closes
paywallModal.heroFeature2: Know exactly how much you gained or lost, and why
paywallModal.heroFeature3: Never get caught off guard by news — we alert you before it moves your money
paywallModal.heroFeature4: Your portfolio built in 30 seconds, no spreadsheets
paywallModal.heroFeature5: 5 concrete opportunities every week, with the reason and the risk
paywallModal.feature1Text: Ask whatever you want, whenever you want — no limit
paywallModal.feature1Detail: An AI mentor available 24/7 that knows your real portfolio and answers with context on what you actually own, not in the abstract.
paywallModal.feature2Text: Be the first to know when one of your companies is about to report
paywallModal.feature2Detail: Before quarterly results, the AI explains what to expect and if there's anything you should care about. No surprises.
paywallModal.feature3Text: Find out if your money would survive another crisis like 2008 or COVID
paywallModal.feature3Detail: Simulate your current portfolio against 30 real historical crises. See how much you would have lost and if you'd be comfortable with that.
paywallModal.feature4Text: The AI honestly tells you what's wrong with your portfolio
paywallModal.feature4Detail: Excessive concentration in one sector, lack of diversification, positions inconsistent with your profile. Concrete, not generic.
paywallModal.feature5Text: Upload your account statement and the AI builds everything itself
paywallModal.feature5Detail: Upload a photo or PDF from your broker. The AI automatically detects your stocks, quantities, and purchase prices.
paywallModal.feature6Text: Every Monday, 5 investment ideas designed for your situation
paywallModal.feature6Detail: Not generic ideas. The AI considers what you already own, your profile, and what's happening in the market that week.
paywallModal.feature7Text: Find out what's happening with your stocks before it affects your money
paywallModal.feature7Detail: Notifications when something important moves one of your positions — with an explanation of why and whether you should do something.
paywallModal.feature8Text: At the close of each month, know if you beat the market
paywallModal.feature8Detail: Your real return vs. the S&P 500. A personalized note on what worked and what didn't. No excuses, no technical jargon.
paywallModal.feature9Text: Learn about the companies you already have in your portfolio
paywallModal.feature9Detail: Lessons designed for what you actually own. Before Apple reports, understand what to watch. Relevant, not generic.
paywallModal.feature10Text: See how your portfolio compares to the market in real time
paywallModal.feature10Detail: A simple dashboard: your performance today, this week, this year — against the S&P 500. Always know if you're doing well.
paywallModal.feature11Text: Add as many stocks as you want, no cap
paywallModal.feature11Detail: The free plan has a limit of 10. Premium removes that limit so your portfolio is fully yours, complete.
paywallModal.feature12Text: Nuvos gets to know you better over time — and remembers everything
paywallModal.feature12Detail: With Premium you build your complete financial memory: behavior patterns, decision timeline, and portfolio evolution. The free plan only stores 10 basic beliefs.
paywallModal.subheadline: The support you need to make your money work for you — every day.

nuvosGuide.s1Description: Ask any question about stocks, markets, strategies, or your portfolio. It shows real fundamentals — P/E, margins, cash flow — and teaches you to think like an investor.
nuvosGuide.s1Tip: 15 free messages a day. Start with: 'What is an ETF and how does it work?'
nuvosGuide.s2Subtitle: Track your investments in real time
nuvosGuide.s2Description: Add your positions and see your total return, P&L per asset, and current value. Deep AI analysis (stress test, sector distribution, comparison vs. S&P 500) is Premium.
nuvosGuide.s2Tip: Add even one position to activate real-time tracking.
nuvosGuide.s3Subtitle: Follow the stocks you care about
nuvosGuide.s3Description: Monitor up to 25 companies for free with real-time prices, daily change, and price alerts. No limit with Premium.
nuvosGuide.s3Tip: Add companies you use every day: Apple, Amazon, Google...
nuvosGuide.s4Subtitle: Learn to invest step by step
nuvosGuide.s4Description: 12 complete categories: from what a stock is to fundamental analysis, investor psychology, and advanced strategies. Free with no limit.
nuvosGuide.s4Tip: 5 minutes a day is enough. The streak keeps you motivated.
nuvosGuide.s5Subtitle: What the world's best investors say
nuvosGuide.s5Description: Short clips of Warren Buffett, Ray Dalio, Peter Lynch, and more. Filter by topic (value, macro, psychology) or by investor. Completely free.
nuvosGuide.s5Tip: Search 'panic' or 'crisis' to see how the best think in tough moments.
nuvosGuide.s6Description: Enter starting capital, monthly contribution, and expected return. See your 30-year projection: how much you accumulate, how much is yours vs. the market, and your financial goal.
nuvosGuide.s6Tip: Try $200/month at 8% annual for 20 years. The result is surprising.
nuvosGuide.s7Subtitle: 5 opportunities every Monday based on your profile
nuvosGuide.s7Description: Every Monday the AI selects 5 assets with real fundamentals: catalyst, 0-100 score, risk analysis, and a personalized note from your mentor. Premium only.
nuvosGuide.s8Description: Deep analysis: sector distribution, 0-100 risk score, stress test with 5 historical crises, and comparison vs. S&P 500. Premium only.
nuvosGuide.s9Subtitle: Connect your broker or import from PDF
nuvosGuide.s9Description: Upload your account statement and AI extracts everything automatically. Or connect Fidelity, Schwab, IOL, or other brokers for automatic syncing. Premium only.
nuvosGuide.s10Description: At month's end: real return, comparison vs. S&P 500, most profitable positions, and a personalized note from your mentor. Premium only.
nuvosGuide.drawerSubtitle: Everything you can do on the platform

metricHints.capMkt: Total value of all shares in the market. Measures how big the company is.
metricHints.ev: Real price to buy the entire company: market cap + debt − cash.
metricHints.peTtm: How many years of current earnings you're paying for each share. A high P/E means the market expects a lot of future growth.
metricHints.peFwd: P/E using next year's expected earnings. Reflects the market's expectations.
metricHints.peg: P/E adjusted for expected growth. Below 1 can suggest the stock is undervalued.
metricHints.ps: Price divided by annual sales. Useful for companies that aren't profitable yet.
metricHints.pb: Market price vs. book value. Below 1 can suggest it's trading below its book value.
metricHints.evEbitda: Company valuation vs. its operating profitability. Lower is generally cheaper.
metricHints.evRevenue: Similar to P/S but uses the full enterprise value, including debt.
metricHints.grossMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left after paying the direct cost of producing or selling.
metricHints.opMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left after all of the business's operating expenses.
metricHints.netMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left as final profit after taxes and all costs.
metricHints.ebitdaPct: Profitability before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Shows pure operating potential.
metricHints.roe: How much the company earns for every dollar of shareholder capital. The higher, the more efficient.
metricHints.roa: How much the company earns for every dollar of assets it has. Measures how well it uses its resources.
metricHints.revGrowth: How fast sales grew compared to the same period last year.
metricHints.earningsGrowth: How fast earnings grew compared to the same period last year.
metricHints.freeCashFlow: Real cash the company generates after its investments. More reliable than accounting earnings.
metricHints.debtEquity: How much debt it has for every dollar of its own capital. Lower is financially safer.
metricHints.currentRatio: Ability to pay its short-term debts. Above 1 is a good sign.
metricHints.quickRatio: Like the Current Ratio but excluding inventory. More conservative and demanding.
metricHints.cash: Cash on hand and available liquid assets.
metricHints.totalDebt: Sum of all the company's debt, both short and long term.
metricHints.bookValue: Book value per share according to the balance sheet.
metricHints.sma50: Average price over the last 50 days. If the price trades above it, it's a short-term bullish signal.
metricHints.sma200: Average price over the last 200 days. The key long-term trend reference.
metricHints.beta: Volatility relative to the market. Beta > 1 means it moves more than the S&P 500, for better and worse.
metricHints.shortPct: Percentage of shares traders are betting will fall. Very high can indicate risk or a possible 'short squeeze'.
metricHints.shortRatio: Days it would take short sellers to cover their positions. More days = more potential upward pressure.
metricHints.shares: Total shares outstanding in the market.
